description_long: This is a tall, rectangular shelving unit made primarily of dark wood, featuring four open shelves. The shelves are filled with various items, including green glass bottles, rolled scrolls, books, a mortar and pestle, and small jars, suggesting it could be used for storage or display in an urban environment such as a workshop, garage, or community center. The sides and back are solid, with no visible openings or doors, and the shelves are evenly spaced. The surface of the wood has a matte finish with subtle grain patterns visible, giving it a sturdy and utilitarian appearance. The bottom shelf contains books and what appear to be small boxes or containers, while the upper shelves hold bottles and other miscellaneous objects. There are no visible handles, hinges, or glass panels, indicating a simple, functional design.
description: A tall, dark wooden shelving unit with four open shelves, filled with bottles, books, scrolls, and jars. The design is utilitarian and sturdy, suitable for storage or display in urban settings.
description_view_0: Front view showing all four shelves filled with bottles, books, scrolls, jars, and a mortar and pestle.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the solid, flat wooden side panel with no openings or features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing the solid back panel with visible wood grain, no openings or features.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, identical to the other side, showing a plain, solid wooden panel.
